items.author: James P.
items.title: Survived a 2m drop onto concrete
items.body: Dropped this off scaffolding onto the slab below. Picked it up, not a scratch. Screen fine, everything working. My old Samsung would have been dust. Battery easily lasts two full days with normal use.

items.author: Mark T.
items.title: Night vision camera is brilliant
items.body: The night vision camera on this is genuinely useful. I do a lot of work in loft spaces and under floors, and being able to see clearly in the dark without a torch in one hand is a game changer. Build quality is solid.

items.author: Sarah K.
items.title: Great phone for outdoor work
items.body: Using this for my landscaping business. Rained on it all day, no issues. Screen is bright enough to read in sunlight. Only reason for 4 stars is it is a bit heavy compared to a normal phone, but that is the trade off for the protection.

items.author: Dave R.
items.title: Finally a phone that lasts the shift
items.body: 10600mAh battery is no joke. I leave for site at 6am, heavy use all day with photos, calls, WhatsApp, GPS. Still at 40% when I get home at 6pm. The 33W charging is fast too, tops it up in about an hour.

items.author: Chris M.
items.title: Replaced my third cracked iPhone
items.body: I have been through three iPhones in two years on site. Decided to try this. Six months in, looks brand new despite living in my tool belt. The rubber corners absorb everything. Should have done this years ago.

items.author: Paul W.
items.title: Perfect size for the van
items.body: Fits in the van door pocket, which is exactly what I needed. Use it for digital job sheets, Google Maps, and taking photos of completed work. Screen is easy to read in direct sunlight. Battery lasts two days easily.

items.author: Tom H.
items.title: Best tablet for site work
items.body: We use these on our construction sites for snagging lists and progress photos. The screen works with gloves on which is essential in winter. IP67 means rain is not a worry. Bought three for the team.

items.author: Andy L.
items.title: Solid but takes some getting used to
items.body: Coming from an iPad this is obviously heavier and the screen is not as sharp. But it survives things that would kill an iPad instantly. The 4G connectivity works well on remote sites where WiFi is not an option. Good value.

items.author: Steve G.
items.title: Mounted in the van, brilliant
items.body: Got the vehicle mount with this and it sits on my dashboard perfectly. GPS is accurate, screen is bright, and the 4G means I can pull up job details anywhere. The 10000mAh battery means I never have to worry about it dying.

items.author: Rachel N.
items.title: Good for field inspections
items.body: I use this for property inspections. Camera is decent for photos, the screen is big enough to fill in forms, and I do not have to worry about dropping it. Delivery took a while but the product itself is solid.

items.author: Mike D.
items.title: Great for blueprints on site
items.body: The 10 inch screen makes a real difference when viewing PDF plans and blueprints. Bright enough to see outdoors. Dual SIM 4G is handy. The battery lasts a full shift with heavy use. Much cheaper than the enterprise alternatives I was looking at.

items.author: John B.
items.title: Solid budget option
items.body: For £299 you are getting a lot of tablet. IP68, big battery, 4G, decent screen. It is not going to compete with an iPad on speed or screen quality but for site work it is more than good enough. And it actually survives the job.

items.author: Lisa F.
items.title: Replaced our fleet of iPads
items.body: We had four iPads that kept breaking. Replaced them all with these. Three months in, zero damage, zero downtime. The total cost was less than two of the iPads. Should have switched sooner.

items.author: Gary S.
items.title: Perfect for warehouse inventory
items.body: Using this in our warehouse for stock counts and goods in. Big screen makes it easy to see the spreadsheets. Handles dust and the odd drop onto the warehouse floor without any drama. Face unlock works well too.

items.author: Emma C.
items.title: Good value, good build
items.body: Bought this for my partner who is a plumber. He needed something waterproof that could handle being in a tool bag all day. This does the job. The HD+ screen could be sharper but for the price it is fine.

items.author: Neil J.
items.title: Windows in your pocket, genuinely
items.body: I need to run specific Windows software for my job. This lets me do that without carrying a laptop. The Intel N100 handles everything I throw at it. 16GB RAM and 512GB SSD means no compromises.

items.author: Alan W.
items.title: Does what it promises
items.body: Runs full Windows 11 Pro, fits in a holster, and survives the rain. The screen is small for Windows but that is the whole point of this form factor. I use it for quick data entry in the field and it works perfectly for that.

items.author: Karen P.
items.title: Niche but excellent
items.body: This is not for everyone but if you specifically need Windows software on a handheld device, there is nothing else at this price point. We use it for our legacy asset management system that only runs on Windows. Saves carrying a laptop to every site.

items.author: Brian H.
items.title: Perfect for meter reading
items.body: Our meter reading software only runs on Windows. These handhelds are exactly what we needed. Rugged enough for all weather, the barcode scanner variant is accurate, and the battery lasts a full route. Ordered five more.

items.author: Simon R.
items.title: Proper Windows tablet for the field
items.body: Finally a Windows tablet that does not cost over a grand. Runs all my engineering software, the HDMI output means I can connect to site monitors, and the USB A port is a lifesaver for plugging in USB drives. Rugged build is solid.

items.author: Laura M.
items.title: Great for diagnostics
items.body: I am an HVAC engineer and this runs all my diagnostic software perfectly. The pogo pin dock is great for when I am at the desk. Only slight issue is the battery could be bigger for long days, but I just bring a power bank.

items.author: Rob K.
items.title: The ports sold me
items.body: USB C, USB A, HDMI all on an 8 inch tablet. That is what I needed. I plug into machinery for diagnostics and having proper ports instead of dongles saves time. Build quality is excellent, feels like it could survive anything.

items.author: Helen T.
items.title: Replaced our expensive enterprise tablets
items.body: We were paying over £1000 each for similar spec tablets from a big brand supplier. Found these, same specs, same protection ratings, fraction of the price. No regrets. The team prefers them actually.

items.author: David A.
items.title: Full desktop replacement on site
items.body: The 10 inch screen and full Windows means I can run CAD, spreadsheets, and our project management software all on one device. The RJ45 Ethernet port is something you just do not find on tablets anymore. Brilliant for connecting to site networks.

items.author: Peter N.
items.title: Surveying made easier
items.body: Using this with our surveying software and GPS. The big FHD screen makes a huge difference compared to our old 8 inch tablets. Fingerprint sensor is a nice touch for quick unlocking with clean hands. Battery handles a half day, which is fine with a car charger.

items.author: Ian G.
items.title: Heavy but capable
items.body: At 1.1kg this is not something you hold one handed for long. But on a desk, mounted in a vehicle, or with the hand strap it is perfectly fine. The screen quality and processing power make up for the weight. Full Windows means full software compatibility.

items.author: Claire B.
items.title: Worth every penny
items.body: Bought this for our quality control team. They can run our inspection software, take photos, generate reports, and email them all from one device. The barcode scanner option is excellent for inventory work. Very happy with the purchase.
